required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root
Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters)
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Reference DOM (Asterisk in sibling span): Required marker asterisk lives in sibling span, not inside label text Reference DOM (Submit outside form): Submit button outside form linked via form= attribute Reference DOM (Custom div dropdown): Location picked from custom div dropdown, not native select Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: shadow-host, duplicate-name-attrs, aria-label-only, pill-yes-no, decoy-form. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Asterisk in sibling span; Submit outside form; Custom div dropdown.
